sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: American Water Marks 140 Years of Safe, Reliable Water and Wastewater Service to Customers and Communities in 2026 – Company Announcement - FT.com
feed.summary: American Water is commemorating 140 years of providing safe, clean, reliable, and affordable water and wastewater services. The company plans to invest up to $48 billion in infrastructure upgrades and regulated acquisitions over the next decade. American Water also recognized the 15th anniversary of its Charitable Foundation and the 45th anniversary of its Research & Development Program.

feed.title: Jack Henry & Associates: Quality Remains, But Growth Is Slowing (NASDAQ:JKHY)
feed.summary: Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY) demonstrates stable operational momentum with strong Q2 revenues and EPS, yet it faces structural growth headwinds. The company's business model is characterized by 90% recurring revenues and high margins, but its single-digit revenue growth and modest EPS growth limit its upside. Despite a current valuation of 24x P/E, which is a discount to historical multiples, the valuation is still considered elevated for a defensive, low-growth profile, prompting a neutral stance on the stock.

feed.title: How Amex exploits new AI tools
feed.summary: American Express is extensively integrating artificial intelligence across its operations, identifying "hundreds" of use cases in areas like sales, engineering, and customer service. CEO Steve Squeri highlighted that AI tools help Amex travel advisors provide faster recommendations, engineers trim coding times by over 30%, and sales teams generate leads and automate follow-ups. Despite widespread concerns about AI-driven job losses, Amex emphasizes that its AI adoption focuses on enhancing tools and deepening customer relationships rather than significantly reducing headcount.

feed.title: Dow Rallies 375 Points On Gains For Walt Disney, Travelers Shares
feed.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rallied 375 points, driven by gains in shares of Walt Disney and Travelers. This news article also briefly covers Unity Software's fourth-quarter financial results, where the company reported a 35% year-over-year revenue increase to $609 million, surpassing analyst estimates, despite a quarterly loss of 66 cents per share.

feed.title: Verizon to post quarterly results April 27 before 8:30 a.m. webcast
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. (NYSE, Nasdaq: VZ) announced it will report first-quarter 2026 earnings on Monday, April 27, 2026. A webcast of the results will begin at 8:30 a.m. ET, with accompanying materials available on Verizon's Investor Relations website starting at 7:00 a.m. ET. The company also disclosed that it generated $138.2 billion in revenue for 2025.

feed.title: Fastenal Company Announces Conference Call to Review 2026 First Quarter Earnings
feed.summary: Fastenal Company (Nasdaq:FAST) has announced a conference call to discuss its 2026 first-quarter financial results and current operations. The live webcast is scheduled for Monday, April 13, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. central time, with an accompanying presentation available at 6:00 a.m. central time. An online archive of the call will be accessible shortly after its conclusion until June 1, 2026.

feed.title: Important Notice to Long-Term Shareholders of BellRing
feed.summary: Grabar Law Office is investigating potential breaches of fiduciary duty by officers and directors of BellRing Brands, Inc. (NYSE: BRBR), Coty Inc. (NYSE: COTY), Gartner, Inc. (NYSE: IT), and Ramaco Resources, Inc. (NASDAQ: METC). Shareholders who purchased shares before specific dates and still hold them may be eligible to seek corporate reforms and other remedies. The investigations stem from alleged materially false or misleading statements and failures to disclose adverse facts about the companies' business, operations, and prospects.

feed.title: Form 424B2 CITIGROUP INC
feed.summary: Citigroup Global Markets Holdings Inc., guaranteed by Citigroup Inc., is offering Buffered Autocallable Securities due May 5, 2031, linked to the S&P 500 Futures 40% Intraday Edge Volatility TCA 6% Decrement Index (USD) ER. These debt securities do not pay interest, may be automatically redeemed early at a premium, and expose investors to potential principal loss if the underlying index depreciates beyond a 20% buffer. The underlying index is highly risky due to leveraged exposure to the S&P 500 Futures Excess Return Index, subject to notional costs and a 6% annual decrement, which can significantly underperform the S&P 500 Index.

feed.title: Microsoft Stock Decline Amid Cloud Competition & AI Risks | 2026 Analysis - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Microsoft (MSFT) shares recently dropped seven percent in a week and over 25% year-to-date despite strong Q2 financial results, including a 17% revenue increase and 26% growth in Microsoft Cloud revenue. The decline is attributed to intensifying cloud competition, with Google Cloud's revenue growth accelerating faster than Azure, and long-term risks posed by AI's rapid evolution to Microsoft's software-as-a-service business model.

feed.title: Earnings Preview: What To Expect From Rollins' Report
feed.summary: Rollins, Inc. (ROL), a global pest and wildlife control service provider with a $25.3 billion market cap, is projected to announce Q1 2026 adjusted EPS of $0.24, an increase of 9.1% year-over-year. Despite lagging behind the S&P 500 and the Industrial Select Sector SPDR ETF over the past year, analysts hold a "Moderate Buy" rating for ROL stock, expecting a potential upside of 23.4% from current levels. The company's Q4 2025 results saw a decline in shares due to weaker-than-expected revenue and profitability, primarily attributed to erratic weather affecting higher-margin work.

feed.title: 3 Companies Built Their Fortunes on COVID Vaccines, but Only 1 Has a Real Plan for What Comes Next
feed.summary: This article examines three companies that initially made fortunes from COVID-19 vaccines: Moderna (MRNA), Novavax (NVAX), and BioNTech (BNTX). While all faced challenges post-pandemic, the author argues only BioNTech has a robust plan for future growth, primarily through a strong oncology pipeline backed by a substantial balance sheet. Moderna shows recent momentum but struggles with cash burn, while Novavax is profitable but faces high volatility and dependence on partnerships.

feed.title: Declining Equity and Credit Markets in 2026 Will Affect T. Rowe Price's Flows and AUM Levels
feed.summary: Morningstar believes that while T. Rowe Price faces challenges from declining equity and credit markets in 2026, it is well-positioned among US-based traditional asset managers. The company's strengths include its operational scale, strong brands, ability to deliver above-average active fund outperformance, reasonable fees, and a sticky client base primarily from retirement accounts. Despite potential market headwinds, T. Rowe Price's fundamental business strategy is expected to help mitigate the impact on its flows and assets under management.

feed.title: MORNSTAR: Blackstone's Private Credit Exposure Creates Headwinds for Fundraising and Will Increase Redemptions
feed.summary: Blackstone, a preeminent alternative-asset manager with $1.275 trillion in assets under management, faces headwinds due to its significant private credit exposure. This exposure is expected to create challenges for fundraising efforts and lead to increased redemptions. The company's fair value estimate has been lowered by 17% as a result of these private credit concerns.

feed.title: Papa Johns Turns Up the Heat on Menu Innovation with Bold New Launch: Oven‑Toasted Sandwiches
feed.summary: Papa Johns has debuted new Oven-Toasted Sandwiches, marking an expansion beyond pizza into a new menu category. The company aims to redefine the sandwich market with higher-quality ingredients and an indulgent experience. This launch signifies a further evolution for the brand, emphasizing its "better ingredients" promise.

feed.title: QQQI ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns BOATS:QQQI
feed.summary: The NEOS Nasdaq 100 High Income ETF (QQQI) is an actively managed ETF that invests in Nasdaq-100 Index stocks, using NDX call options to generate high monthly income. It focuses on tax efficiency through Section 1256 Contracts and aims for tax-optimized tracking of the index. As of March 26, 2026, QQQI has significant exposure to Electronic Technology and Technology Services, with top holdings including NVIDIA Corporation and Apple Inc., and offers a dividend yield of 15.42%.

feed.title: Coca-Cola Stock Is Up 10% in 2026. Here’s Why Analysts See a $90 Target
feed.summary: Coca-Cola's stock has risen 10% in 2026, with analysts setting a $90 target, implying a 19.2% upside over 2.8 years. This is driven by defensive investor demand, strategic investments in high-growth brands like Fairlife, and marketing initiatives like the FIFA World Cup. Despite strong operational performance and profitability, the stock's current valuation, trading at a premium, leaves less room for error, with future growth dependent on sustained earnings and managing cost pressures.

feed.title: Exxon Mobil, Pepsico among market cap stock movers on Monday
feed.summary: This article highlights major stock movers on Monday, March 30, 2026, categorizing them by market capitalization. Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Pepsico (PEP) are noted among the mega-cap stocks that are rallying, while others like Micron Tech (MU) are falling. The report also details movements in large-cap, mid-cap, and small-cap stocks, including specific company news and analyst rating changes impacting their performance.

feed.title: Why Are Micron Technology Shares Sliding On Monday?
feed.summary: Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) shares fell after Google unveiled TurboQuant, an AI memory compression algorithm, which analysts believe could reduce the demand for memory chips. While some analysts are concerned about "demand destruction," others suggest efficiency gains could lead to increased consumption. The article also notes that Monday is the ex-dividend date for Micron's quarterly payout.

feed.title: Wabtec Announces First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release Date
feed.summary: Wabtec Corporation will release its first quarter 2026 financial results on Friday, April 25, 2026, followed by a conference call with investors and analysts. This announcement is significant for those monitoring the transportation industry, as Wabtec's performance is often seen as an indicator of the sector's health and future trends. Investors and analysts will closely watch the report for insights into the company's outlook and the broader transportation technology sector.

feed.title: Corteva panelists to present Hertz Lecture at Iowa State April 14
feed.summary: Three senior leaders from Corteva Agriscience — Geoff Graham, Jeremy Dirks, and Mark Deterding — will participate in the 2026 Carl and Marjory Hertz Lecture on Emerging Issues in Agriculture at Iowa State University on April 14. They will discuss Pioneer's historical role in the seed industry, its partnership with Iowa State, and its evolution to meet farmer needs. The event is free, open to the public, and offers scholarship opportunities for attending students.

feed.title: PepsiCo Could Climb 10% to $169 as Deutsche Bank Rates the Stock a Buy
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank maintains a Buy rating for PepsiCo (PEP) with a $169 price target, representing a 10% upside potential despite recent stock pullbacks. The company's consistent dividend increases, international growth, and strategic investments are key drivers. Reaching this target hinges on organic revenue growth, EPS growth, and stabilization of North American volumes, though consumer trade-down and commodity headwinds pose risks.

feed.title: Jack in the Box names CMO to strengthen product innovation, loyalty
feed.summary: Jack in the Box has appointed Katelyn Zborowski as its new Chief Marketing Officer, aiming to enhance product innovation and customer loyalty. Zborowski, who previously spent over a decade at Yum Brands (Taco Bell, Pizza Hut), is credited with developing numerous limited-time offerings that boosted sales. Her appointment comes as Jack in the Box navigates a challenging quick-service restaurant environment marked by price-sensitive consumers and increased competition.

feed.title: Spotlight on Central Florida: A conversation with Scott Cathcart, Florida Regional President at Truist
feed.summary: Scott Cathcart, Florida Regional President at Truist, discusses the strong business momentum in Central Florida, driven by population growth and sector diversification. He highlights key opportunities like access to capital for acquisitions and challenges such as talent availability and high insurance costs. Cathcart also details how Truist is partnering with businesses in the region, focusing on relationship-driven advisory models and supporting local economic development.

feed.title: CMS Energy (CMS) COO receives stock award and withholds shares for taxes
feed.summary: CMS Energy's Executive Vice President & COO, Tonya L. Berry, received a grant of 2,021 shares of common stock after the company exceeded performance criteria. Concurrently, 2,642 shares were withheld at $76.33 per share to cover tax obligations. Following these routine transactions, Berry directly holds 70,105 CMS shares, which also includes an adjustment for dividend reinvestment.

feed.title: CMS Energy (NYSE: CMS) SVP gains shares via award as taxes withheld
feed.summary: CMS Energy Corp's Senior Vice President, Lauren Y. Snyder, received 674 shares of Common Stock as a performance-based Restricted Stock Award on March 26, 2026. Simultaneously, 574 shares were withheld at $76.33 each to cover tax obligations. After these transactions and 70 shares from dividend reinvestment, her direct holdings increased to 16,596 CMS shares.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Stock Is Up 8% Over the Past Year. Here’s What Could Drive the Path to $328
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) stock has seen an 8% increase over the past year, with a valuation model targeting $328, implying a 72.5% upside. This potential growth is supported by strategic product announcements, such as collaborations with NVIDIA for AI data centers and humanoid robots, and the recent $7.5 billion acquisition of Silicon Labs. Investors are currently weighing these developments against pending Q1 earnings and the long-term integration of the Silicon Labs deal.

feed.title: CMS Energy (NYSE: CMS) CEO granted stock; shares withheld for tax
feed.summary: CMS Energy Corp's CEO, Garrick J. Rochow, was granted 19,681 shares of Common Stock on March 26, 2026, after the company exceeded performance criteria for a 2023 Restricted Stock Award. On the same date, 25,833 shares were disposed of at $76.33 per share to cover tax obligations. Following these transactions, Rochow directly holds 637,918 shares of CMS Common Stock, indicating routine incentive plan activity.

feed.title: [144] Keysight Technologies, Inc. SEC Filing
feed.summary: This article announces an SEC Form 144 filing by Keysight Technologies, Inc. ([KEYS]). The filing indicates a proposed sale of 870 shares of common stock acquired through restricted stock vesting on March 20, 2026, with an approximate sale date of March 30, 2026. The filing has been assessed as having a neutral impact and sentiment.

feed.title: Meta Stock Is Having a Rough Year. Why It’s a ‘Top Pick’ at Morgan Stanley.
feed.summary: Meta Platforms (META) stock has experienced a 20% decline in the first quarter of 2026 due to negative headlines, yet Morgan Stanley considers it a "top pick." This indicates a potential disconnect between current market performance and analyst sentiment. The article suggests that despite a rough start to the year, there might be underlying reasons for optimism from a major financial institution.

feed.title: General Motors boosting production of heavy-duty trucks to meet demand (GM:NYSE)
feed.summary: General Motors (GM) is increasing the production of its heavy-duty pickup trucks by adding an extra production day at its Flint, Michigan plant. This move aims to boost output by an additional 1,100 vehicles per day to meet strong demand. The expansion will specifically target the Chevy Silverado series.

feed.title: Ferrari has spent €217M buying back shares since Jan. 5
feed.summary: Ferrari (NYSE: RACE) has reported buying back 737,600 of its own shares for a total of €217,227,659.06 since January 5, 2026, as part of the first tranche of its multi-year share buyback program. As of March 27, 2026, the company held 17,382,206 treasury shares, representing 8.96% of total issued common shares. These purchases contribute to a larger multi-year program expected to reach approximately €3.5 billion by 2030.

feed.title: Piper Sandler lowers Fifth Third Bancorp price target to $54
feed.summary: Piper Sandler has lowered its price target for Fifth Third Bancorp (NASDAQ: FITB) to $54 from $57, while maintaining an Overweight rating. This adjustment reflects a revised expectation for the bank to resume share repurchases in the fourth quarter of 2026, later than previously anticipated, and generally lower bank equity valuations. Despite the lowered target and some downward revisions to EPS estimates by analysts, Piper Sandler still views the stock favorably, citing potential cost savings and revenue opportunities from the recent Comerica acquisition.

feed.title: Salesforce Bets on Data 360: Will It Power CRM's Next Growth Cycle?
feed.summary: Salesforce is heavily investing in its Data 360 platform (formerly Data Cloud) to drive its next phase of growth, integrating it with existing tools like Agentforce and Mulesoft to help companies manage and activate data in real time, especially for AI applications. This strategy aims to reaccelerate revenue growth, which has slowed to single digits recently, with the company estimating Data 360 could generate $10 billion in annual revenues by fiscal 2027. Despite facing competition from Microsoft and Snowflake and a recent stock price decline, Salesforce's Data 360 platform is showing solid momentum, bringing in significant recurring revenues and improving earnings estimates.

feed.title: Can NVIDIA's Data Center Business Sustain Its High Growth Momentum?
feed.summary: NVIDIA's data center business has shown tremendous growth, fueled by strong demand for accelerated computing and AI technologies, with record revenues of $62.31 billion in Q4 fiscal 2026. The company anticipates continued strength in the near term with new product shipments and expanding orders, while competitors like AMD and Intel are also making strides in the AI data center space. Analysts project significant revenue and earnings growth for NVIDIA in the coming fiscal years, with the stock currently holding a "Strong Buy" rating.

feed.title: Piper Sandler reiterates Best Buy stock rating at Neutral, $68 PT
feed.summary: Piper Sandler has reiterated a Neutral rating on Best Buy (NYSE:BBY) with a $68 price target, noting the stock currently trades below both their target and InvestingPro's Fair Value. The firm expressed increased optimism after recent investor meetings, citing mobile phone launch timing for Q1 sales and potential for new RGB LCD TV technology in June to boost TV sales. Despite acknowledging past product innovation challenges for Best Buy, Piper Sandler remains Neutral, awaiting more certainty on TV market trends.

feed.title: Wall Street Is Bearish on General Motors Stock
feed.summary: General Motors stock has seen recent declines, though Barclays analyst Dan Levy maintains an Overweight rating and a $105 price target, implying a 44% upside. Despite headwinds like Q1 2026 tariff costs, GM's strong adjusted EPS guidance, capital returns, North America margin recovery, and growth in Super Cruise revenue are key drivers for its valuation. The company's Q1 2026 earnings report will be a critical test for management's guidance.

feed.title: Aptiv PLC - Attractive In A Problematic Sector (NYSE:APTV)
feed.summary: Aptiv (APTV) is rated 'Buy' with a $90/share price target, implying it is undervalued with potential for a 20% annualized return despite facing margin compression and volatile markets. The company's future growth hinges on software and EV adoption, but current revenue growth is dampened by declining margins and weak cash flow. While the valuation is attractive, alternatives like Autoliv and Magna are suggested as they offer similar upside, dividends, and stronger balance sheets.

feed.title: Devon Energy (NYSE:DVN) Reaches New 52-Week High on Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Devon Energy (NYSE:DVN) reached a new 52-week high after Morgan Stanley raised its price target from $46 to $59, maintaining an "overweight" rating. The stock closed at $52.07 and traded as high as $52.52 with a volume of 92,475 shares. Analysts have a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and a target price of $50.32, with the company recently beating EPS estimates and declaring a quarterly dividend of $0.24.

feed.title: Canada awards $1.5B in aircraft-support contracts to L3Harris, Airbus (LHX:NYSE)
feed.summary: Canada has awarded approximately $1.5 billion in long-term support contracts to L3Harris Technologies and Airbus. These contracts are for the maintenance and technical support of Canada's new CC-330 Husky aircraft fleet. The move is part of a broader government initiative to enhance the country's defense capabilities.

feed.title: Serving 14 million people, American Water turns 140 in 2026
feed.summary: American Water (NYSE: AWK) is celebrating its 140th anniversary in 2026, marking decades of service to over 14 million people across 14 regulated states and 18 military installations. The company plans to invest up to $48 billion over the next decade in infrastructure upgrades, resiliency, and regulated acquisitions. This milestone also highlights the 15th anniversary of its Charitable Foundation, which has provided over $25 million in grants, and the 45th anniversary of its R&D program.

feed.title: ServiceNow’s Armis Deal Recasts AI Platform Around Security And Capital Choices
feed.summary: ServiceNow announced its largest acquisition to date, an all-cash deal of $7.75 billion for cybersecurity firm Armis, significantly expanding its AI platform into security. This strategic move, along with recent partnerships, aims to position ServiceNow as a comprehensive AI-powered control layer for workflows, agents, and security. The acquisition, however, comes amid a challenging share price performance for ServiceNow and raises questions regarding capital allocation and integration risks for investors.

feed.title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC has issued $958,000 Auto Callable Contingent Interest Notes linked to the MerQube US Tech+ Vol Advantage Index, fully and unconditionally guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co. These notes offer contingent interest payments if the index level meets certain criteria, with an automatic call feature and potential for principal loss if conditions are not met. The article details the terms, risks, and tax considerations associated with investing in these structured notes.

feed.title: Amgen’s Repatha Slashes Heart Event Risk in Early‑Stage Patients
feed.summary: Amgen's drug Repatha has shown significant efficacy in reducing the risk of heart events in patients with early-stage cardiovascular disease. This development could broaden the drug's application beyond its current use and potentially transform treatment protocols for at-risk individuals. The findings strengthen the case for aggressive LDL-C reduction in preventing cardiovascular incidents.

feed.title: Sprouts’ CEO says the chain needs to be a better ‘value’ for shoppers
feed.summary: Sprouts Farmers Market is re-evaluating its approach to “value” after disappointing comparable-store sales growth and transaction volume in fiscal year 2025. CEO Jack Sinclair clarified that this focus on value isn't about deep price cuts, but rather about showcasing the relevance and quality of its differentiated product assortment, particularly in health-focused and organic categories. The company is also investing in a new loyalty program for customer insights and expanding its self-distribution network to improve gross margins and affordability.

feed.title: 5 Stocks to Buy Before Q2 Heats Up
feed.summary: Morningstar's Chief US Market Strategist, Dave Sekera, and Susan Dziubinski discuss their market outlook for Q2, identifying undervalued sectors and specific stock picks. Despite a generally undervalued US market, Sekera advises caution and portfolio readjustments, focusing on high-quality stocks with wide economic moats. The article highlights five undervalued stocks: GE Healthcare (GEHC), LPL Financial (LPLA), Invitation Homes (INVH), Energy Transfer (ET), and Masco (MAS).

feed.title: Sanofi’s Sarclisa subcutaneous formulation administered via on-body injector recommended for EU approval by the CHMP to treat multiple myeloma
feed.summary: Sanofi's Sarclisa subcutaneous (SC) formulation, administered through an on-body injector (OBI), has received a positive opinion from the European Medicines Agency’s CHMP for EU approval in treating multiple myeloma. This recommendation is based on studies demonstrating comparable efficacy, pharmacokinetics, and safety to the intravenous formulation. If approved, Sarclisa SC would be the first anticancer treatment available via OBI and manual injection for multiple myeloma in the EU, offering increased flexibility and patient satisfaction.

feed.title: Sunstone Hotel Investors Inc To Go Ex-Dividend On March 31st, 2026 With 0.09 USD Dividend Per Share
feed.summary: Sunstone Hotel Investors Inc (SHO.US) will trade ex-dividend on March 31st, 2026. Shareholders of record on that date will receive a cash dividend of $0.09 per share on April 15th, 2026. The article also provides general definitions of cash and stock dividends, noting that the company's actual implementation is subject to final disclosure.

feed.title: Booking Holdings Fell 6% This Week. Here’s Where the Stock Could Go in 2026
feed.summary: Booking Holdings (BKNG) shares fell 6% this week after Sanford C. Bernstein lowered its price target, amidst investor discussions on sustained global travel demand. Despite strong operating performance in 2025 and positive 2026 guidance, the stock is considered undervalued by TIKR's model, with an implied upside of 54% driven by continued travel demand, strong margins, and increased monetization. CEO Glenn Fogel and Rep. Gilbert Cisneros also made recent share sales, which may have impacted short-term confidence.

feed.title: Sigma Lithium Corporation 2025 Q4 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation (NASDAQ:SGML) 2026-03-30
feed.summary: Sigma Lithium Corporation published its Q4 2025 earnings call presentation. The article, authored by SA Transcripts, highlights the availability of the slide deck for investors interested in the company's financial results. Sigma Lithium Corporation trades under the ticker SGML on NASDAQ.

feed.title: Deere taps startups to explore sensing, AI tech
feed.summary: John Deere has selected five companies for its 2026 Startup Collaborator Program, which aims to explore emerging technologies in sensing, analytics, and robotics. This initiative connects Deere with hand-selected startups for year-long collaborations to test new technologies in agricultural and construction scenarios, focusing on areas like soil analysis, equipment monitoring, and robotics. While not primarily an investment or acquisition vehicle, the program seeks to identify innovative solutions that could offer significant value to Deere's customers.
